items.prompt: A permutation solver builds a path of chosen values. When is a complete permutation ready to record?

options.text: When path length equals n.
As soon as the path contains one value.
Whenever the last value differs from the previous value.
Only when the current index is zero.

feedback.reason: A permutation is complete only after every input position or value has been assigned exactly once.
feedback.details: The completion condition must reflect the result contract, not merely that some progress occurred.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.path_nonempty: A partial path is not a complete permutation.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.no_duplicate_value: Local inequality does not establish completion.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.index_zero: The index value does not define a completed assignment.

items.prompt: A combination solver chooses exactly k values. Which completion condition is correct?

options.text: Record a result when the current path contains k values.
Record a result only when the candidate start reaches n.
Record immediately after the first choice.
Record whenever the current loop has no candidates.

feedback.reason: The base case must encode the exact required output size.
feedback.details: Candidate exhaustion is a failure or dead-end condition unless the required number of values has already been selected.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.start_equals_n_only: The scan may reach n with fewer than k selected values.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.first_choice: That records incomplete combinations when k is greater than one.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.no_choices: A dead end may be incomplete and invalid.

items.prompt: A grid word search has matched wordIndex characters. When is success complete?

options.text: When wordIndex equals the target word length.
Whenever the path reaches a grid edge.
Only after every grid cell has been visited.
Whenever the current cell has no legal neighbor.

feedback.reason: Success is defined by consuming the entire target, not by a geometric property of the grid.
feedback.details: Once all characters are matched, no further move is required.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.at_grid_edge: Grid position does not determine whether the word is fully matched.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.visited_all_cells: The target may be shorter than the number of cells.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.no_neighbors: A dead end is not success unless the word is complete.

items.prompt: A partition solver processes a string using a start index and appends valid segments to a path. When is one full partition complete?

options.text: When start equals the string length.
When the path contains at least one segment.
Whenever the most recent segment is valid.
When the next boundary is before the end.

feedback.reason: A partition is complete only when the chosen segments cover the entire input exactly.
feedback.details: Using an exclusive start boundary makes start == length the natural completion condition.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.path_nonempty: A non-empty path may still leave an unprocessed suffix.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.segment_valid: One valid segment does not complete the entire input.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.end_less_length: That indicates remaining input, not completion.

items.prompt: A collect-all solver appends the current mutable path directly to results without copying it. What defect is likely?

options.text: Later mutations can change every stored result that refers to the same path object.
Appending the path automatically removes duplicate values.
The recursion can no longer return.
The stored path is automatically sorted.

feedback.reason: A completed result must preserve a snapshot of the path at that moment.
feedback.details: When the same mutable path object is reused, result storage should receive a copy or another immutable representation.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.recursion_stops: The reference bug does not prevent control flow.

items.prompt: A find-one solver discovers a valid complete branch. Which result action matches the contract?

options.text: Return success and propagate it through callers so the search can stop.
Always append the solution and continue exploring every branch.
Immediately erase the fact that success was found.
Restart the search from the root.

feedback.reason: A boolean existence contract permits short-circuiting on the first complete valid branch.
feedback.details: The base case and recursive callers must agree on how success is represented and propagated.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.append_and_continue_all: That implements collect-all rather than find-one.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.undo_success: Restoration does not mean discarding the result.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.restart_root: The requested existence result is already known.

items.prompt: For a solver returning all subsets, what result should the empty input produce?

options.text: A collection containing one empty subset.
An empty collection of subsets.
An error because no choice can be made.
A subset containing a null placeholder.

feedback.reason: The empty construction is a valid completed subset when the task enumerates all subsets.
feedback.details: This illustrates why an empty input can correspond to one solution rather than zero solutions, depending on the result contract.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.no_subsets: The empty set is itself a valid subset.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.error: Zero choices can still define one completed empty construction.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_null_value: No placeholder is part of the mathematical result.

items.prompt: A partial assignment violates a mandatory constraint before all variables are assigned. How should that state be classified?

options.text: A failure state that returns without recording a result.
A complete result because the branch cannot continue.
A result that should be recorded as a partial solution.
A signal to erase every result found so far.

feedback.reason: Completion and dead-end conditions must not be conflated.
feedback.details: A branch can terminate because it is impossible, because it is complete, or because the result contract allows short-circuit success. Each case needs distinct behavior.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.successful_completion: Inability to continue does not imply validity.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.record_partial: The contract requires complete valid assignments.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.restart_all: One failed branch does not invalidate sibling results.

items.prompt: Why can placing a completion check only after generating children be incorrect?

options.text: A complete state may need to be recorded before any further candidate loop runs.
Every complete state always has children.
Base cases affect only speed, never correctness.
Recursive functions cannot return before a loop.

feedback.reason: A completed path is itself a result node and may intentionally have no outgoing choices.
feedback.details: Checking completion at function entry makes the contract explicit and prevents candidate generation from skipping terminal results.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.children_always_empty: Complete states often intentionally stop generating children.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.base_cases_only_optimize: Incorrect base-case placement can omit or duplicate results.

items.prompt: Which conditions can be legitimate backtracking base cases? Select all that apply.

options.text: The partial state satisfies the full result contract.
The partial state cannot lead to any valid completion.
A requested find-one result has already been found.
The branch appears expensive but may still contain valid solutions.

feedback.reason: Backtracking termination can represent success, failure, or contract-driven short-circuiting.
feedback.details: Each base case needs a precise semantic outcome. An expensive-looking branch is not automatically removable.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.branch_looks_large: Cost alone is not a sound termination condition.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.complete_solution: A valid completed state must be emitted or reported.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.impossible_state: An impossible state terminates as failure.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.find_one_success: Short-circuit contracts can terminate further search.

items.prompt: Which actions are required when recording a completed mutable path? Select all that apply.

options.text: Store a copy or immutable snapshot of the current path.
Do not add an unrelated extra choice merely to mark completion.
Either continue or short-circuit according to collect-all versus find-one semantics.
Clear previously recorded sibling results.

feedback.reason: Recording must preserve the completed state and obey the requested result semantics.
feedback.details: Mutable path reuse and mismatched find-one versus collect-all behavior are common contract defects.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.clear_all_results: A new valid solution does not invalidate earlier solutions.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.copy_path: A snapshot preserves the completed result.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.avoid_extra_choice: The current path already represents the result.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.follow_result_contract: The caller contract determines control flow after recording.

items.prompt: Which empty-input outcomes can be correct depending on the stated contract? Select all that apply.

options.text: One empty construction for all-subsets or all-partitions style enumeration.
False when an empty board must match a non-empty target.
Zero valid assignments when the contract requires assigning at least one missing variable.
An error for every backtracking problem with empty input.

feedback.reason: Empty input has no universal answer across backtracking problems.
feedback.details: The correct result follows from the mathematical identity, completion condition, and target requirements.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_error: Empty-input semantics depend on the result contract.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.one_empty_construction: The empty construction can be a valid result.

items.prompt: Arrange the control flow for a collect-all backtracking call.

elements.text: If complete, record a snapshot and return from this branch.
Recurse for each choice and restore branch state.

feedback.reason: Failure and completion are resolved before expanding nonterminal states.
feedback.details: This ordering prevents invalid states from expanding and ensures completed states are recorded exactly once.

items.prompt: Arrange the steps for recording a completed mutable path safely.

elements.text: Verify that the completion contract is satisfied.
Create a snapshot of the current path.
Append the snapshot to the results.
Return from the completed branch without generating extra choices.

feedback.reason: A result must be recognized, frozen, stored, and then treated as terminal.
feedback.details: Copying after later mutations would capture the wrong state, and continuing choices could create invalid overlong results.

items.prompt: Arrange the checks for a find-one grid word-search call.

elements.text: If the full target is matched, return success.
Reject out-of-bounds, mismatched, or path-reused cells.
Mark the current cell and search legal neighbors.
Restore the cell state and return failure if no neighbor succeeds.

feedback.reason: Success completion is checked before attempting another cell, while invalid current states fail before mutation.
feedback.details: The order separates completed success, invalid failure, recursive expansion, and branch restoration.

items.prompt: A collect-all solver records s solutions, each containing k values, by copying the current path at completion. Select the unavoidable result-copy work and output space.
constraints: Each completed result is stored independently.
The path copy contains k values.

feedback.reason: Materializing s independent length-k results requires copying and storing sk values.
feedback.details: This cost is output-sensitive and cannot be removed when the contract requires all results explicitly.
